standard_description: Compare two decimals to thousandths based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
subject: Math

problems.text:
Problem 1
Determine if each comparison is true or false. Record your answer with an X in the table below.
###TABLE0###
Problem 2
Four people competed in a high jump competition. The table below shows each person’s best height.
High Jump Competition
###TABLE1###
List the heights in order from the least to greatest.

Problem 1
Would you rather have Option A or Option B? Explain.
###TABLE0###
Problem 2
Compare each of the following, recording the result of your comparison using <, >, or =. Show or explain your thinking.
a.   0.045 and 0.318
b.   0.705 and 0.7
c.   15.406 and 15.43
d.   0.560 and 0.56
Problem 3
List the following numbers in order from least to greatest:
###TABLE1###
Problem 4
Compare two hundred seven thousandths and
$$\left(2\times\frac{1}{10}\right)+\left(6\times\frac{1}{100}\right)$$
. Record the result of your comparison using <, >, or =.

Task
Which is greater, 0.01 or 0.001? Explain. Draw a picture to illustrate your explanation.
Which is greater, 0.03 or 0.007? Explain. Draw a picture to illustrate your explanation.
Which is greater, 0.025 or 0.052? Explain. Draw a picture to illustrate your explanation.
Which is greater, 0.13 or 0.031? Explain. Draw a picture to illustrate your explanation.
Which is greater, 0.203 or 0.21? Explain.  Draw a picture to illustrate your explanation.

metadata.title: Drawing Pictures to Illustrate Decimal Comparisons
